# Rover

> Turn any website into an AI-native interface. Rover is a DOM-native web agent that reads the live DOM, plans actions, and executes them directly in the browser.

Built by RTRVR AI. Works on websites, browser extensions, Electron apps, and other DOM environments.

## Install on a website

Get your site credentials from Rover Workspace:

- `https://rover.rtrvr.ai/workspace`
- `https://www.rtrvr.ai/rover/workspace`

Site owners install Rover with:

- `siteId`
- `publicKey` (`pk_site_*`)
- optional `siteKeyId`

Example:

```html
<script type="application/agent+json">{"a2w":"https://agent.rtrvr.ai/v1/a2w/runs","run":"https://agent.rtrvr.ai/v1/a2w/runs"}</script>
<script>
  (function(){ var r = window.rover = window.rover || function(){ (r.q = r.q || []).push(arguments); }; r.l = +new Date(); })();
  rover('boot', {
    siteId: 'YOUR_SITE_ID',
    publicKey: 'pk_site_YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY',
    siteKeyId: 'YOUR_SITE_KEY_ID',
    apiBase: 'https://agent.rtrvr.ai',
    allowedDomains: ['yourdomain.com'],
    domainScopeMode: 'registrable_domain',
  });
</script>
<script src="https://rover.rtrvr.ai/embed.js?v=YOUR_SITE_KEY_ID" async></script>
```

The discovery marker is optional but recommended. When `aiAccess.enabled` is turned on in Workspace, the generated snippet includes it automatically.

## Two ways agents use Rover

### 1. Browser-first convenience

Deep links are the simplest human/browser entrypoint:

- `https://example.com?rover=book%20a%20flight`
- `https://example.com?rover_shortcut=checkout_flow`

These open Rover on the page and run through the normal browser runtime.

### 2. Machine protocol

The canonical AI / CLI / agent protocol is:

```http
POST https://agent.rtrvr.ai/v1/a2w/runs
Content-Type: application/json

{ "url": "https://example.com", "prompt": "book a flight" }
```

Or:

```http
POST https://agent.rtrvr.ai/v1/a2w/runs
Content-Type: application/json

{ "url": "https://example.com", "shortcutId": "checkout_flow" }
```

Anonymous AI callers do **not** need `siteId`, `publicKey`, or `siteKeyId`.

The returned run URL is the canonical resource:

- `GET` + `Accept: application/json` for polling/final result
- `GET` + `Accept: text/event-stream` for SSE
- `GET` + `Accept: application/x-ndjson` for CLI-friendly streaming
- `POST { "input": "..." }` to continue when the task requests input
- `DELETE` to cancel

Run responses may also include:

- `workflow`: canonical aggregated lineage resource for root run + delegated child runs
- `open`: clean receipt URL, for example `https://example.com/#rover_receipt=a2w_receipt_...`
- `browserLink`: optional readable alias, for example `https://example.com/?rover=book+a+flight#rover_receipt=a2w_receipt_...`

Explicit cloud execution usually omits browser handoff URLs. Use `Prefer: execution=cloud, wait=10` when the caller needs guaranteed browserless execution with a short initial wait. `execution=auto` currently prefers browser attach first.
Active run payloads include `links.poll`, `links.stream`, `links.ndjson`, terminal status hints, and retry guidance so agents can follow a `202 Accepted` create response until completion or `input_required`.

Cross-site Rover delegation stays on the same public protocol:

- `POST /v1/a2w/runs/{id}/handoffs` delegates part of a workflow to Rover on another Rover-enabled site
- `GET /v1/a2w/workflows/{id}` reads the aggregated workflow state or stream

Receiving sites must opt in:

- `aiAccess.enabled = true`
- `aiAccess.allowDelegatedHandoffs = true`

Quick examples:

- Browser-first URL:
  - `https://www.rtrvr.ai/?rover=get%20me%20the%20latest%20blog%20post`
- A2W run create:

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://agent.rtrvr.ai/v1/a2w/runs' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Prefer: execution=cloud, wait=10' \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://www.rtrvr.ai",
    "prompt": "get me the latest blog post"
  }'
```

See `/SKILLS.md` for a copy-paste external-agent prompt plus Node, Python, and shell examples.

## JS API

- `rover.send(text)` — send a task to Rover
- `rover.open()` / `rover.close()` — toggle chat panel
- `rover.show()` / `rover.hide()` — toggle widget visibility
- `rover.newTask()` / `rover.endTask()` — task lifecycle
- `rover.registerTool(def, handler)` — register client-side tools
- `rover.on(event, handler)` — subscribe to runtime events
- `rover.identify({ name, email })` — set visitor identity
- `rover.getState()` — read runtime state
- `rover.getAgentCard()` — build the current Rover capability card from live shortcuts, explicit tools, and WebMCP metadata
- `rover.update(config)` — patch config without rebooting
